title: Fractures

steps.title: Check for danger signs
steps.instruction: Call 101 immediately if: the person is unresponsive or not breathing, there is heavy bleeding, the bone is sticking out through the skin, the limb appears deformed, or you suspect a fracture of the head, neck, or spine.

steps.title: Do not move the person
steps.instruction: Keep the person as still as possible. Do NOT try to straighten the bone or push a bone back in. Moving the injured area can cause further damage to nerves, blood vessels, and tissue.

steps.title: Stop any bleeding
steps.instruction: If there is an open wound, apply firm pressure using a sterile bandage, clean cloth, or clothing. Do not press directly on the bone if it is protruding — press around it instead.

steps.title: Immobilize the injured area
steps.instruction: Have the person rest without moving or straightening the injured area. If trained and medical help is delayed, apply a splint above and below the fracture. Pad the splint to reduce pain. Do NOT attempt to realign the bone.

steps.title: Apply a cold pack
steps.instruction: Wrap ice or a cold pack in a thin towel and apply it to the injured area to reduce swelling and pain. Apply for no more than 20 minutes at a time. Never apply ice directly to the skin.

steps.title: Watch for shock
steps.instruction: If the person feels faint, has pale or clammy skin, or is breathing rapidly — lay them down with their head slightly lower than the trunk. Raise the legs if possible. Keep them warm with a blanket.

steps.title: Check circulation
steps.instruction: Check the hand or foot beyond the injury for temperature, color, and feeling. Ask if there is any numbness or tingling. Cold, blue, or numb fingers or toes may indicate a serious injury to blood vessels.

steps.title: Keep them still until help arrives
steps.instruction: Reassure the person and keep them calm and warm. Do not give food or water. Monitor breathing and responsiveness. Stay with them until emergency services arrive.

warnings: Do NOT try to straighten or realign the bone
Do NOT push a bone that is sticking out back in
Do NOT apply ice directly to the skin
Do NOT move the person if head, neck, or spine injury is suspected
sources: American Red Cross First Aid Guidelines
